reasoning: Query is Spanish asking for cherry-blossom (sakura) spots in Tohoku region; tool fetched Akita spots with no sakura filter. Spots returned (Akita Castle, Lake Tazawa, Mt. Chokai, Omagari Fireworks) are real Akita data but cherry-blossom intent is entirely unaddressed — no sakura_meisho_100 kind filtering and no Tohoku region fan-out to include Iwate/Miyagi/Yamagata/Aomori. Rule D applies: query is Spanish but response is JSON with ja/en names, so constraint_handling drops 1 from otherwise 3. Main failure is retrieval: wrong scope (Akita-only instead of Tohoku) and wrong filter (no sakura).
missing_data: Sakura-tagged spots across all 6 Tohoku prefectures (e.g. Hirosaki Castle, Kakunodate samurai district, Kitakami Tenshochi, Funaoka Castle); spring-season timing context.
improvement_hint: Add region fan-out for 'Tohoku' query with sakura_meisho_100 kind filter; classify Spanish queries and apply RULE D language note in tool_scope_note.

reasoning: Query asks for major sake breweries in Niigata; tool returns 24 items via get_local_specialty with sake_brand/sake_brewery kind_tags. Key brands (越乃寒梅, 八海山, 久保田 — the famous 越の三梅trio) are present with Wikidata provenance. Response includes MAFF GI items (Kurosaki Chamame edamame) which are off-topic for sake, adding minor noise — hence intent 4 not 5. Practical usefulness is 4 because brewery physical addresses and visit hours are absent. No language constraint was specified; en lang respected.
missing_data: Physical addresses / visiting hours for breweries; Asahi Shuzo (久保田) and Hakkaisan brewery tasting-room URLs.
improvement_hint: Add brewery-visit practical fields (address, hours, tour availability) to wikidata sake brewery records; suppress pure food-GI items when kind_tags filter = sake_brand/sake_brewery.

reasoning: Query is French asking for ski resorts WITH hot springs (onsen) in Hakuba. Tool returns get_spots for Nagano/白馬 — correctly scoped to Hakuba. Hakuba Happo-One ski resort is present (ski_resort kind), but no onsen/hot-spring data is returned among the truncated spots. Crucially the response is in ja/en, not French: Rule D applies — constraint_handling drops 1 from an already weak 3 to 2. The compound ski+onsen constraint is only half-satisfied. Municipal scrape noise item included. Specificity 3 per RULE C (right location, partial topic coverage).
missing_data: Onsen spots/ryokan in Hakuba (e.g. Mimizuku no Yu, Hakuba Sarukura Onsen); French-language descriptions.
improvement_hint: For ski+onsen compound queries over Hakuba, chain get_hotels with onsen_ryokan kind for the same municipality; add French as output lang to multilingual description layer.

reasoning: Query asks for best autumn foliage (紅葉) spots in Kansai region; tool fetches Kyoto spots with no koyo filter and no Kansai fan-out (Osaka, Nara, Hyogo, Shiga excluded). Returned spots (Nijo Castle, To-ji, Nagaoka-kyo) are generic Kyoto attractions with no autumn-foliage filtering. Per RULE B the primary failure is retrieval scope: no koyo_meisho kind or autumn-season context, and region limited to Kyoto only. constraint_handling 3 because English lang was respected and no other constraints specified. specificity 3 per RULE C — real authoritative data, just wrong topic filter.
missing_data: Koyo-tagged spots across Kansai (Arashiyama, Eikan-do, Minoo Park in Osaka, Korankei-adjacent, Shiga/Biwako spots); peak-season timing.
improvement_hint: Apply koyo_meisho_100 kind filter and enable Kansai region fan-out when query contains 紅葉/autumn foliage + Kansai.

reasoning: Query is Arabic asking for famous cherry-blossom (sakura) spots in Kansai. Tool fetches Nara spots. First result is Mount Yoshino (吉野山) with sakura_meisho_100 kind — a perfect Kansai sakura hit. Koriyama Castle also has sakura association. However: (1) query is Arabic but all output is ja/en — RULE D applies, constraint_handling -1 from 3 → 2; (2) scope is Nara only, missing Osaka (Osaka Castle Park), Kyoto (Maruyama), Hyogo — but Yoshino is arguably the most iconic Kansai sakura site so intent_understanding 4. Per RULE B the language failure is the single worst dim.
missing_data: Arabic-language descriptions; Kansai fan-out for sakura spots in Kyoto, Osaka, Shiga.
improvement_hint: Detect Arabic queries and surface Arabic name/description fields; add region fan-out for Kansai sakura queries.

reasoning: Query is Japanese asking for stargazing spots on Okinawa's remote islands (離島). Tool returns prefecture-level Okinawa spots. Nakagusuku Castle, Tamaudun, Kumejima are returned — Kumejima (a remote island, Ramsar Wetland) is marginally relevant but not tagged as a dark-sky/stargazing site. No dark_sky kind filter was applied and no remote-island filter was used. The stargazing intent is entirely unaddressed in the returned data. Primary failure is retrieval (no dark_sky kind, no island sub-filter). Japanese lang constraint respected.
missing_data: Dark-sky/stargazing-tagged spots on Okinawan islands (Iriomote-Ishigaki, Taketomi, Yonaguni, Miyako); island filter to exclude Okinawa main island.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky kind to spot taxonomy and map Okinawa离島 queries to island-municipality filter + dark_sky kind.

reasoning: Query is Thai asking for rural onsen accommodation in Hokkaido. Tool returns get_hotels for Hokkaido, correctly surfacing hito_yu_kai (日本秘湯を守る会) members — 知内温泉旅館, 銀婚湯, 湯元凌雲閣, 湯元湧駒荘 — which are genuinely remote/rural onsen ryokan. Data is authoritative and highly relevant. However query is Thai and no Thai descriptions are returned (only ja/en) — RULE D: constraint_handling -1 from 3 → 2. Specificity 4 because hito_yu_kai records include Japanese street addresses. Truncation cuts before full list.
missing_data: Thai-language descriptions; coordinates for hito_yu_kai properties (currently null); ANA Holiday Inn in results is urban/non-rural noise.
improvement_hint: Add Thai (th) to multilingual description layer; filter out urban chain hotels when 田舎/rural intent detected; add geocoding for hito_yu_kai records.

reasoning: Query is Vietnamese asking about islands in the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海). Tool fetches Kagawa spots. Top results are Ritsurin Garden and Mannō-ike — mainland Kagawa attractions, not islands. Although Kagawa administers Seto Inland Sea islands (Naoshima, Shodoshima, Ogijima), none appear in the truncated output. The island intent is unaddressed. missing_data: Seto Inland Sea island spots: Naoshima (art island), Shodoshima (olive/Kojo-ji gorge), Teshima, Ogijima; also Hiroshima-administered Onomichi/Itsukushima.
improvement_hint: Add island kind filter and Seto Inland Sea region cluster; fan out across Kagawa+Hiroshima+Okayama for Seto Inland Sea queries.

Awa Shijira Weave is directly on-topic; description_ja is null but description in en is absent too (translation_meta source=official_only, no description). missing_data: Actual indigo-dyeing workshop venues (e.g. Awa Ai-no-Yakata, Fujii Denim); description for Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave (currently null); museum/experience venues tagged with awa_ai/indigo_dyeing kinds.
improvement_hint: Add workshop/experience venue entities with indigo_dyeing kind tag; populate description for Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave record.

reasoning: Query asks for off-the-beaten-path samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area with 武家屋敷 returns 153 hits including Buke Yashiki (Shimane, buke_yashiki kind), Samurai Residence Uchiyama (Fukui), and notably Kakunodate samurai district (Akita) — an Important Preservation District with weeping cherry detail. reasoning: Query is Indonesian asking for summer festivals in Akita. Tool uses get_festivals with lang=id — correctly recognizing Indonesian and returning Indonesian descriptions. Only 3 items returned (Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival, Tsuchizaki Shinmei Shrine Festival) — all from bunka_intangible, which are important intangible folk properties, not typical summer matsuri. The famous Akita Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり, one of Japan's 3 great Tohoku summer festivals) is absent. Date field is null for most items. RULE D: lang=id was passed and descriptions ARE in Indonesian — constraint_handling should be 3 for language. However the key summer festival (Kanto) is missing, causing intent 3 and coverage failure. Adjusting: constraint_handling 3 for lang, but failure category F because summer-matsuri is unaddressed.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり, early August, UNESCO ICH); Omagari Fireworks Competition (August); summer timing/date info for returned items.
improvement_hint: Include scraped_schema_event summer matsuri in get_festivals results; prioritize Kanto Festival for Akita summer queries.

reasoning: Query is Korean asking for small fishing port towns (漁港の町) in San'in region. Tool returns Shimane spots. missing_data: Fishing port town spots: Oki Islands, Sakaiminato (Tottori, famous for fresh fish), Hashi port, Nima coast; Korean descriptions.
improvement_hint: Add gyoko/fishing_port kind tag and fan-out San'in region across Shimane+Tottori for coast/fishing-port queries.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area with 砂丘 returns 91 results with diverse prefectures: Tottori (expected, prominent), Chiba (Onjuku Beach), Hokkaido (Ishikari dune museum), Kagoshima (Fukiagehama, nagisa_100 listed), and others in truncated output. reasoning: Query is Filipino (Tagalog) asking for traditional coffee towns in Kyushu (tradisyonal na bayan ng kape = traditional coffee towns). Tool returns Oita spots. Coffee towns in Kyushu likely refers to places like Morotsuka (Miyazaki, famous for 木城えびの高原 coffee culture) or Amami-Oshima coffee production areas — extremely niche content not in the dataset. Returned spots (Usuki Castle, Usa Jingū, Oka Castle) are unrelated to coffee. Query is Filipino, output is ja/en — RULE D: constraint_handling -1. This may also be a misinterpretation: 'tradisyonal na bayan' = traditional towns, not necessarily coffee-specific. Either way, Oita historic towns are partially related to traditional townscapes but miss the coffee angle. Per RULE C authoritative Oita data merits groundedness 3 not 2, but intent mismatch is severe.
missing_data: Coffee production/café culture spots in Kyushu; if intent is traditional townscapes: Hita (日田) machi, Usuki traditional district; Filipino descriptions.
improvement_hint: Add coffee kind tag for regions with known specialty coffee (Miyazaki, Amami); improve Filipino-language query intent classification.

reasoning: Query asks for kominka (古民家) accommodations to stay in the Hokuriku region. Tool fetches get_hotels for Ishikawa only. reasoning: Query asks about mountain shrines on the Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage for Wakayama returns 7 Japan Heritage stories plus a large browse_advisory block. The browse_advisory includes canonical_tools for 山岳信仰/修験道 and references Kumano — which is directly on-point for Kii mountain shrines. However the full 7 Japan Heritage items are truncated before they appear in the response; we see only the tool_scope_note and browse_advisory. The advisory itself is helpful guidance pointing to search_area/get_traditional_arts. English lang respected. Coverage failure: actual heritage content not visible, only meta-navigation. intent 4 because advisory correctly signals Kumano/山岳信仰 relevance.
missing_data: The 7 Wakayama Japan Heritage story titles and descriptions; direct Kumano Kodo / Yoshino-Kumano links; specific shrine names (Kumano Hongu Taisha, Nachi, Hayatama).
improvement_hint: Reduce browse_advisory verbosity so actual heritage items appear before truncation; add featured_cluster for Kii Peninsula mountain shrines.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along the Sea of Japan coast. get_festivals for Niigata returns 129 items. Third item is 長岡まつり大花火大会 (Nagaoka Grand Fireworks, Aug 2-3) — one of Japan's three great fireworks festivals, directly on-topic. But the first two items (Mushrooms cherry blossom festival in May, Himesayuri festival in May) are spring/flower events unrelated to summer fireworks. No hanabi filter applied; 129 results include substantial non-fireworks noise. Scope is Niigata only — query asks 'Sea of Japan coast' which spans Niigata, Ishikawa, Akita, Yamagata. Coverage failure: only one prefecture, mixed event types without fireworks prioritization.
missing_data: Fireworks festivals across Sea of Japan coast prefectures: Akita Kanto (not fireworks but summer), Noshiro Nebuta, Omagari Fireworks (Akita); Niigata Nagaoka is present but buried.
improvement_hint: Add hanabi event_type filter to get_festivals; enable Sea of Japan coast region fan-out across relevant prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. get_local_specialty craft for Oita returns 2 items. Item 1 is Hakata Ori (textile weaving) — a multi-prefecture craft that includes Oita in its production area but is primarily Fukuoka; tangential to bamboo. Item 2 is Beppu Bamboo Crafts (別府竹細工) — exactly what the query asks for, with detailed en description of Madake bamboo and 8 weaving techniques, METI designated. see_also_wikidata_venues adds museum context. English lang respected. Count of only 2 items is noted but Beppu Bamboo Crafts is the canonical answer. intent 4 not 5 because Hakata Ori adds minor noise.
missing_data: Bamboo craft workshop/experience venues in Beppu; additional bamboo products if any exist in the dataset.
improvement_hint: Rank bamboo-specific craft first when bamboo keyword is in query; remove cross-prefecture crafts (Hakata Ori) where production area for target prefecture is secondary.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Tool fetches Ehime spots. Among visible results: Ōmishima (island) has cycling_route and walking_road_500 kinds — directly relevant. Uwajima Castle and Yuzuki Castle are unrelated to cycling. The Shimanami Kaido cycling route is arguably the most famous cycling route in Shikoku/Setouchi but is not prominently visible. Scope is Ehime only; rural Shikoku cycling also spans Kochi (Shimanto River), Tokushima, Kagawa. English lang respected. Coverage: cycling_route kind present but sparsely tagged and single prefecture only.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaido cycling route data; Shimanto River cycling route (Kochi); Shikoku cycling pilgrimage route context; multi-prefecture Shikoku fan-out.
improvement_hint: Add cycling_route kind filter and Shikoku region fan-out; create featured_cluster for Shimanami Kaido cycling entries.

reasoning: Query asks where to see traditional kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. get_traditional_arts returns a cross_program_advisory with a canonical performing-arts-venues block listing: Konpira Kabuki venue 金丸座 (Kotohira, Kagawa, oldest shibaigoya 1835, April festival), 内子座 (Uchiko, Ehime, ICP), 八千代座 (Yamaga, Kumamoto, ICP), 康楽館 (Kosaka, Akita, ICP) — all outside Tokyo/Kyoto, all with QIDs and rich context notes. English lang respected. Expression 4 not 5 because the advisory block is verbose with subcategories that are off-topic (crafts, tea ceremony); the kabuki venues are buried in the first featured_subcategory but well-structured once found.
missing_data: Practical visit info for venues (season-specific performance dates, ticket booking); Uchiko-za booking URL.
improvement_hint: Surface the performing-arts-venues block earlier when kabuki keyword detected; add season/performance schedule fields to canonical venue entities.
